sections.title: Engineering & Discrete Mathematics
sections.topics: Discrete Mathematics: Propositional and first-order logic, Sets, relations, functions, partial orders, lattices, Groups, Graphs (connectivity, matching, coloring), Combinatorics. Engineering Math: Linear Algebra (Matrices, eigenvalues), Calculus (Limits, maxima/minima), Probability (Random variables, distributions, Bayes Theorem).

sections.title: Digital Logic
sections.topics: Boolean algebra, combinational and sequential circuits, minimization, number representations, and computer arithmetic (fixed and floating-point).

sections.title: Computer Organization and Architecture
sections.topics: Machine instructions and addressing modes, ALU, data path and control unit, instruction pipelining, memory hierarchy (cache, main, secondary), I/O interface (interrupt and DMA).

sections.title: Programming and Data Structures
sections.topics: Programming in C, recursion, arrays, stacks, queues, linked lists, trees, binary search trees, binary heaps, and graphs.

sections.title: Algorithms
sections.topics: Searching, sorting, hashing, asymptotic worst-case time and space complexity, algorithm design techniques (greedy, dynamic programming, divide-and-conquer), graph traversals, minimum spanning trees, shortest paths.

sections.title: Theory of Computation
sections.topics: Regular expressions and finite automata, context-free grammars and push-down automata, regular and context-free languages, pumping lemma, Turing machines, and undecidability.

sections.title: Compiler Design
sections.topics: Lexical analysis, parsing, syntax-directed translation, runtime environments, intermediate code generation, local optimization, and data flow analyses.

sections.title: Operating Systems
sections.topics: System calls, processes, threads, inter-process communication, concurrency and synchronization, deadlock, CPU and I/O scheduling, memory management, virtual memory, and file systems.

sections.title: Databases
sections.topics: ER-model, relational model (relational algebra, tuple calculus, SQL), integrity constraints, normal forms, file organization, indexing (B and B+ trees), transactions, and concurrency control.

sections.title: Computer Networks
sections.topics: Concept of layering (OSI, TCP/IP), switching, data link layer (framing, error detection, MAC), routing protocols, fragmentation, IPv4, NAT, transport layer (TCP, UDP), and application layer protocols (DNS, SMTP, HTTP).
